key_outputs.core_claim: Sparsemax+rationale supervision improves ERASER comprehensiveness over SRA without macro-F1 cost, via structural zero on non-rationale tokens
key_outputs.primary_hypothesis: H1: sparsemax comprehensiveness AOPC gain >= +0.04 over SRA; macro-F1 delta <= ±1.0 (TOST equivalence); 5 seeds
key_outputs.secondary_hypotheses: H2: rationale density validation (<30%), H3: head-selective supervision beats uniform, H4: adversarial swap causality, H5: identity-term FPR reduction
key_outputs.novelty_positioning: vs SRA: structural zero vs. soft penalty; vs Malaviya2018: annotation mask vs. fertility budget

summary: 5 hypotheses generated. H1 is the primary NeurIPS-grade contribution. H4 provides mechanistic validation (Jain-Wallace causal-attention test). H5 provides fairness impact. Novelty confirmed against SRA and Malaviya2018 constrained sparsemax.
critical_context: H1: sparsemax AOPC comprehensiveness >= SRA+0.04; macro-F1 delta <= 1.0 (equivalence test); 5 seeds on HateXplain
H4: adversarial swap KL-divergence >= 2x softmax baseline; proves sparsemax causally constrains computation
H5: FPR on non-hate identity-mention posts; sparsemax <= SRA - 0.03
Kill condition H1: if comprehensiveness gain < +0.02 AOPC OR F1 drops > 1.0, H1 is falsified
datasets.md and references.bib written; primary dataset HateXplain, eval framework ERASER
